generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components (Physical Acting Components)
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driven by current I_M, part of the first axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot. Operates within [-90°, 90°] range with max angular velocity of 75°/s. Does not actively influence physical twin but provides mechanical energy through rotational motion."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor to measure rotational position (r_M) with high precision for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind the gearbox to measure output rotational position (r_out) and enable backlash/friction analysis via differential comparison with motor encoder."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring motor current (I_M) as input for torque calculation and friction modeling."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance Sinumerik840DslController : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller providing access to time-series sensor data (position, velocity, acceleration) via MQTT streaming and enabling derivation of torque/velocity from raw inputs."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ControllerReferenceDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance ControllerReferenceDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom Sinumerik840DslController
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance MotorDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable MATLAB Simulink model converting motor current (I_M) to torque (τ_M) using manufacturer-specific parameters for the permanent magnet synchronous motor."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance GearwheelBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable MATLAB Simulink model simulating ideal gearwheel behavior (without friction) for rotational speed transformation between motor and gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance GearboxBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable MATLAB Simulink model simulating cycloidal gearbox behavior including friction effects (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) for output position (r_out) prediction."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance MotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured motor rotational position (r_M) from motor encoder, used as input for gearwheel and gearbox simulations."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance OutputPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured output rotational position (r_out) from secondary encoder, used for friction model validation and comparison with simulated outputs."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured motor current (I_M) converted to torque (τ_M) using manufacturer-specific parameters, used as input for friction modeling."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance ReferencePositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Reference position, velocity, and acceleration data from SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller used for model validation and deviation analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ControllerReferenceDataTransmitted
]

instance SimulatedMotorOutput : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated motor rotational position (r̂_M) output from friction models, used for comparison with actual measurements to identify friction discrepancies."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorDynamicsModel
]

instance SimulatedGearwheelOutput : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated gearwheel rotational position (r̂_G) output from friction models, used for intermediate friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearwheelBehaviorModel
]

instance SimulatedGearboxOutput : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated gearbox output position (r̂_out) including friction effects, compared against actual secondary encoder measurements."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxBehaviorModel
]

instance FrictionModelParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Dynamic friction model parameters (static, kinetic, viscous coefficients) derived from runtime data for continuous model refinement."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML (Block Definition Diagrams and Internal Block Diagrams) used for structural and behavioral modeling of the first axis components and their energy flows."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink environment implementing executable friction and dynamics models for real-time simulation and analysis."
    DTDFVocab:hasInput MotorDynamicsModel, GearwheelBehaviorModel, GearboxBehaviorModel
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance MQTTDataStreamingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol enabling real-time streaming of sensor data (encoder angles, motor current) from SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller to digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:hasInput MotorEncoderDataTransmitted, SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted, MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService
]

instance AssetAdministrationShellEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell (AAS) providing contextual metadata and data trace provenance for all processed data traces."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous multi-physics-aware detection of static, kinetic, and viscous friction in the cycloidal gearbox using white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) models."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCharacterizationInsight, ModelRefinementRecommendation
]

instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Computation of higher-level abstractions (torque τ, angular velocity ω) from raw sensor data via simulation models for unified analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides DerivedDataInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integration of processed data into a unified dashboard for real-time monitoring of friction-related deviations between simulated and measured data."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualAnalyticsInsight
]

instance ModelValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Comparison of reference values (from CNC controller) against simulated outputs (r̂_M, r̂_G, r̂_out) to assess friction model accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionCharacterizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantification of motion inaccuracies caused by static, kinetic, and viscous friction in the gearbox, identified as up to 30% of positional error."
]

instance ModelRefinementRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggestions for updating friction parameters (e.g., load-dependent coefficients) based on runtime data to improve model accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DerivedDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Higher-level derived data (torque τ, angular velocity ω) computed from raw sensor inputs for comprehensive friction analysis."
]

instance VisualAnalyticsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dashboard displays deviation trends (r vs. r̂) enabling manual adjustments by operators or engineers to mitigate friction-induced errors."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Assessment of friction model accuracy through comparison of simulated outputs with reference values from the CNC controller."
]

instance ControlStrategyImprovement : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Insights into compensatory control algorithms (e.g., torque profile adjustments) to mitigate friction-induced positional errors."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Early detection of wear patterns or gearbox degradation via friction model deviations, enabling proactive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environment
instance FirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder, MotorCurrentSensor, Sinumerik840DslController
]

instance SimulationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MotorDynamicsModel, GearwheelBehaviorModel, GearboxBehaviorModel, SimulinkSimulationEnabler, SysMLModelingEnabler, MQTTDataStreamingEnabler, AssetAdministrationShellEnabler
]

// C13: Components
instance MotorGearboxAssembly : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical component assembly consisting of permanent magnet synchronous motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox forming the first axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot."
    base:isContainedIn FirstAxisEnvironment
]

instance FrictionModelComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital component implementing both white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models for comprehensive friction analysis."
    base:isContainedIn SimulationEnvironment
]

instance DataProcessingPipeline : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital component processing raw sensor data through simulation models to generate derived outputs for friction analysis and visualization."
    base:isContainedIn SimulationEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates on **real-time and near-real-time scales**:
   - **Live data acquisition**: Sensor data (e.g., encoder angles, motor current) is streamed at **high-frequency intervals** (not explicitly quantified but implied by CNC controller capabilities).
   - **Simulation updates**: Friction models (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) are dynamically recomputed during operation to adapt to **wear, load, or temperature changes**.
   - **Dashboard updates**: Visualization occurs at **user-defined intervals** (not specified) but is synchronized with live data ingestion. The DT **does not synchronize with the PT at fixed intervals** but rather processes continuous data streams for friction identification."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is organized as follows:
   - **Centralized DS**: A single, unified digital shadow representing the **first axis** of the MABI MAX 100 robot, composed of:
     - **Data sources**: Physical sensors (encoders, current sensor) and derived data (e.g., torque from dynamics models).
     - **Processing components**: Simulation models (*Simulation M*, *Simulation GW*, *Simulation GB*) that consume input data traces and produce derived outputs.
     - **Metadata layer**: Links data traces to their origins (e.g., motor encoder, simulation models) and system properties (e.g., time intervals).
   - **No decentralization**: The DS is not partitioned into sub-twins; instead, it integrates **multi-physics models** (mechanical, electrical) into a **single computational pipeline**.
   - **Orchestration**: The DS follows a **purpose-driven workflow**:
     1. **Data ingestion** (MQTT streams from CNC controller).
     2. **Model processing** (Simulink friction/dynamics models).
     3. **Data aggregation** (comparison of measured vs. simulated outputs).
     4. **Visualization** (dashboard for friction analysis).
   The constellation does not include **external DTs** or **horizontal integrations** with other systems."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin covers the **operational phase** of the physical twin's lifecycle, focusing on **as-operated** (runtime) behavior rather than design or manufacturing stages. It does not model:
   - **As-designed** (idealized) behavior (though ideal frictionless models are used for comparison).
   - **As-manufactured** deviations (e.g., geometric tolerances).
   Instead, the DS **continuously adapts** to **operational conditions** (e.g., wear, tool changes) by refining friction models during runtime. The DT is **not retrofitted** post-manufacture but developed **concurrently with the robot's operational phase** for real-time friction analysis."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The DT incorporates the following fidelity and validity considerations:
   - **Model Fidelity**:
     - **White-box models**: Analytical friction models (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) with **known mathematical equations** (referenced in [45]) to describe friction physics.
     - **Black-box models**: Data-driven approaches to avoid parameter identification challenges in complex white-box models.
     - **Simulation models**: MathWorks MATLAB Simulink implements **multi-physics simulations** (mechanical/electrical) with **manufacturer-provided parameters** (e.g., motor constants).
   - **Validation Mechanisms**:
     - **Comparison of measured vs. simulated data**: Deviations between *r* (reference) and *r̂* (simulated) are analyzed to **identify friction loss**.
     - **Uncertainty quantification**: Not explicitly detailed, but implied via **encoder differentials** (motor vs. gearbox encoders) to assess elasticity and friction.
     - **Parameter sensitivity**: White-box models require **parameter tuning** (e.g., load, temperature dependencies), while black-box models reduce this burden.
   - **Error Sources**:
     - **Sensor noise**: Encoder measurements may introduce positional uncertainty.
     - **Model simplifications**: Friction models may not capture all physical effects (e.g., Stribeck effect nuances).
     - **Dynamic changes**: Wear or tool variations may require **runtime model updates**."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT technical connection involves:
   - **Data Protocols**:
     - **MQTT** for **real-time streaming** of sensor data (e.g., encoder angles, motor current) from the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller** to the DS.
     - **No bidirectional communication**: The DS is a **passive entity** (one-way data flow from PT to DS).
   - **Network Architecture**:
     - **Industrial Ethernet** (implied by CNC controller integration) for sensor data transmission.
     - **Centralized processing**: All data is ingested into a **single DS pipeline** (Simulink models + dashboard).
   - **Latency**: Not explicitly quantified, but **near-real-time** due to MQTT streaming and Simulink processing."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted and deployed as follows:
   - **Execution Environment**:
     - **MathWorks MATLAB Simulink** for **simulation model execution**.
     - **Dashboard software** (not specified) for visualization, likely integrated with Simulink or a separate tool (e.g., LabVIEW, Python-based dashboards).
   - **Deployment Architecture**:
     - **Centralized server/PC**: Hosts the DS pipeline, including:
       - **MQTT broker** for data ingestion.
       - **Simulink models** for friction/dynamics computation.
       - **Dashboard application** for real-time monitoring.
     - **No cloud deployment** mentioned; likely **on-premise** for industrial robot integration.
   - **Scalability**: Not addressed; the DS is tailored for the **first axis** of the MABI MAX 100 robot."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to the following standards:
   - **Modeling Standards**:
     - **SysML** (OMG standard) for **systems engineering models** (Block Definition Diagrams, Internal Block Diagrams).
     - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** (IEC 62421) for **contextual metadata** and data trace provenance (implied but not explicitly detailed).
   - **Simulation Standards**:
     - **MathWorks MATLAB Simulink** (industry-standard for multi-physics simulation).
   - **Data Exchange**:
     - **MQTT** (OASIS standard) for **real-time sensor data streaming**.
   - **No explicit compliance** with **IEC 63141** (Digital Twin standard) or **ISO/IEC 42010** (system modeling) is mentioned, though the methodology aligns with **purpose-driven DT design** principles."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to the following security and safety considerations:
   - **Passive Operation**: The DS is a **read-only entity**; it does not execute **remote control actions** on the PT, eliminating risks of **unintended actuation**.
   - **Data Isolation**: Sensor data (e.g., encoder angles) is streamed via **MQTT**, which may require **network segmentation** to prevent unauthorized access.
   - **Model Integrity**: Simulation models (Simulink) are **locally executed**, reducing risks of **malicious model tampering**.
   - **Safety Measures**:
     - The **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller** enforces **safety constraints** (e.g., working range [-90°, 90°], max speed 75°/s).
     - **No remote shutdown** capabilities are implemented in the DS.
   - **Human Safety**: The DS **does not interact with humans** directly; insights are provided for **operator/engineer review** to inform manual adjustments or maintenance."
]
